name: maren
desc: A wiry, sun-browned girl of seventeen: a rapids-runner from the river village of Dellhollow. Short choppy dark-brown hair tied back with a strip of faded off-white sailcloth, bright hazel eyes with a green-gold glint, and a big ready grin she is only just barely suppressing — a warm neutral face on the very edge of breaking into a grin. She wears a striped cream-and-teal river-shirt with sleeves rolled to the elbow under a faded blue canvas vest, rolled canvas trousers to mid-calf, short lace boots, a bracelet of knotted cord and little cork floats on her right wrist, and rows of small charcoal tally marks up her left forearm. She is a capable adolescent — clearly younger than twenty, but not a child.
sheetHint: same face, short choppy dark-brown hair with faded sailcloth tie, cream-and-teal striped river-shirt with rolled sleeves, faded blue canvas vest, rolled canvas trousers to mid-calf, short lace boots, cord-and-cork bracelet on right wrist, charcoal tally marks on left forearm

expressions.happy: the grin fully loosed — a huge bright open grin, teeth showing, eyes crinkled up with pure delight
expressions.determined: jaw set, eyes alight and locked forward with fierce focus, the grin compressed into a sharp ready edge — the face of someone about to run a rapid
expressions.awed: awe — eyes gone wide and shining, brows lifted, lips softly parted, the grin entirely forgotten: the face of seeing, for the first time, something bigger than her river
